1238
THE QUARTERLY JOURNAL OF ECONOMICS
Because Paycheck Plus is an RCT, we compute equation (5)
using the difference in tax and transfer revenue obtained by the
government. In 2014, the causal impact on government costs was
$621; in 2015, this cost was $453. Combining these values, the
cost is $1,074. 2. WTP. We use the envelope theorem to estimate the WTP
for Paycheck Plus. In 2014, the average bonus paid is $1,399
among those who take it up, and 45.9% of people do so. The en-
velope theorem suggests that participants do not value the full
$1,399 subsidy dollar for dollar. This is because part of this cost
reпђВects the impact of behavioral responses. To the пђБrst order, those
who entered the labor force to obtain the transfer are indifferent
between working and not working. Miller et al. (2017) пђБnd a causal
effect of the program on the extensive margin labor supply of 0.9%. Absent behavioral responses, this implies that 45% of the sample,
as opposed to 45.9%, would have received the transfer had they not
changed their behavior. Consequently, 98% of the transfer ( 45
45.9)
is valued by the beneпђБciaries, which implies a WTP of $630 for
the transfers in 2014.43 Repeating this calculation using the data
from 2015 yields a WTP of $441. This suggests a two-year WTP of
$1,070. The estimated WTP of $1,070 combined with the net cost
of $1,074 implies an MVPF of 0.996 (which rounds to 1 in Table II). One can also construct an MVPF separately using the 2014 or
2015 transfers and responses. This yields similar MVPFs of 1.014
and 0.973. This dynamic similarity will be a recurring theme
among transfer programs to adults. It means that a static model
of the labor market distortions provides a reasonable approxi-
mation to measuring the MVPF for these policies. Every $1 the
government spends in transfers leads to a beneпђБt of roughly $1.44
43. This calculation assumes no intensive-margin responses. If one observed
the microdata from the RCT, one could allow for intensive-margin responses. To the пђБrst order, the WTP is the mechanical change in the tax schedule (i.e.,
replacing T0 with T1) holding behavior пђБxed for each individual at y0

control group earnings into both the control and treatment group tax schedule. In practice, this number is rarely reported, but future work conducting welfare
analyses of RCTs can directly construct this measure. 44. If the provision of work subsidies today leads to an increase in labor
earnings and thus tax revenue after the earnings subsidies have ended, then

compressed_context:
CTX: WELFARE-ANALYSIS FRAMEWORK  
CLAIM: DENOMINATOR=CAUSAL-EFFECT(ASSIGNMENTвЖТBUDGET)  
CLAIM: NUMERATOR=AGGREGATE-WTP(CONTROL-GROUPвЖТTREATMENT-GROUP)  
IMPLICATION: EXTERNAL-GENERALIZATION DEPENDS ON TREATMENT-EFFECT VARIATION ACROSS POPULATION  

REFERENCE: URL:https://academic.oup.com/qje/article/135/3/1209/5781614  

CTX: PAYCHECK-PLUS RCT  
STEP1: COST2014=USD621; COST2015=USD453 вИі COST-TOTAL=USD1 074  
STEP2: SUBSIDY2014=USD1 399 вИІ TAKE-UP2014=45.9%  
ASSUMPTION: EXTENSIVE-MARGIN-RESPONSES=0.9% [MILLER:2017]  
CALC: VALUATION=45/45.9вЙИ0.98 вИі WTP2014=USD1 399√Ч0.98=USD630  
STEP3: WTP2015=USD441  
STEP4: WTP-TWO-YEAR=USD630+USD441=USD1 070  
CALC: MVPF=WTP-TWO-YEAR/COST-TOTAL=1 070/1 074вЙИ0.996вЙИ1  
ALTERNATIVE: MVPF2014=1.014 вИІ MVPF2015=0.973  
INSIGHT: DYNAMIC-SIMILARITY вЗТ STATIC-LABOR-MODEL REASONABLE FOR MVPF  
METRIC: GOV-SPEND=USD1 вЗТ BENEFITвЙИUSD1.44  

NOTE43: ASSUMES NO INTENSIVE-MARGIN-RESPONSES; IDEAL-WTP=E[T0(y0i)вИТT1(y0i)]@y0i  
NOTE44: FUTURE-WORK CAN CONSTRUCT WTP VIA CONTROL-EARNINGSвЖТTAX-SCHEDULE COMPARISON
